summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several recent developments involving small UAS. Ukraine has reportedly struck FPV drone training and production sites, as well as a Russian electronic warfare station, while also revamping military training to focus on FPV drone defense. In Iraq, an Iranian-backed militia claimed responsibility for an FPV drone strike on U.S. Victory Base near Baghdad, releasing video showing the drone bypassing U.S. defenses. Russian forces have used FPV drones to strike civilian targets in the Kharkiv region, killing two medics and a woman in Kozacha Lopan, and injuring two workers when a drone hit a bus in Kutsurub. Additionally, residents in the Kharkov region have cut fiber-optic cables used by Ukrainian FPV drones, and Zelensky has warned that Russia has ordered seven million FPV drones.
